details: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the "Bharati Scheme for Education - Graduation (Any regular course of 3 years duration after 10+2 will be considered under this category. i.e., B.A., B.Com, B.Sc., etc., including B.Ed.)" for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts.
benefits: Financial Assistance for the graduation student: ₹15,000/-.
eligibility: The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community.
The student should be studying in any recognized and reputed college in Andhra Pradesh for the academic year.
The student's parents should be staying in Andhra Pradesh.
The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The student should have cleared all the subjects of the previous year.
The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS).

The student should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose. However, students availing benefit from Archaka Welfare Trust are eligible under this scheme.

offline: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name.
Fill out the application form with required details like:
Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc
Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details
Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc
Receive the reference number after the submission.
Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "
Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status.
Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme.
Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme".

documents_required: Aadhar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh)
White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva)
Study Certificate (from the College / Institute for the academic year)
Marks Sheets of all the Previous Years (starting from the senior secondary certificate)
SB Account Passbook in the name of the Applicant (Account should be in Andhra Bank or SBI).

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Bharati Scheme for Education (BSE)?
faqs.answer: Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh with family income less than ₹3,00,000/-.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the BSE?
faqs.answer: To support Brahmin students in continuing their education.

faqs.question: Are part-time courses eligible under BSE?
faqs.answer: No, only regular courses are eligible.

faqs.question: How is eligibility verified?
faqs.answer: Through Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies department data.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit for applicants?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned in the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Can applicants from other states apply?
faqs.answer: No, only students with Andhra Pradesh domicile are eligible.

faqs.question: Can students availing benefits from other government schemes apply?
faqs.answer: Generally not, except for those under Archaka Welfare Trust.

faqs.question: Can students with backlogs apply?
faqs.answer: No, students must have cleared all subjects in previous years.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
faqs.answer: Directly into the student's bank account through online transfer.

faqs.question: What documents are needed for application?
faqs.answer: Aadhar card, income certificate, caste certificate, study certificates, and bank passbook.

faqs.question: How can I apply for BSE?
faqs.answer: Apply online at www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in with required documents.
